description_long: This house features a medieval, half-timbered design with a stone lower level and timber-framed upper story. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with wooden shingles, with a prominent stone chimney rising from the main section. The front facade includes a small, protruding bay window with blue glass panes and a large wooden door beneath. An attached covered area on one side serves as a workspace, complete with a forge, tools, and a stack of firewood, suggesting the house may double as a blacksmith's workshop. Windows are irregularly placed, with some featuring decorative trim and others being simple rectangular openings. The exterior is predominantly gray and brown, with weathered textures and visible wooden beams accentuating the rustic, historic character.
description: A medieval-style, half-timbered house with a stone base, steep shingled roof, and attached blacksmith workshop. Features include a bay window, large chimney, and rustic wooden details.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance, bay window with blue glass, and the attached workshop area with forge and tools.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long, shingled roof, symmetrical window placement, and the stone and timber construction.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back entrance to the workshop, a small window, and the extension of the stone wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the covered workspace, stacked firewood, and a hanging sign, with more visible timber framing.
attribute_entryway_design: Large wooden door beneath a bay window, with a covered porch area and visible stone steps leading to the entrance.
